displayTitle: Star Wars (1977) #38: Riders in the Void
synopsis:
While transporting medical supplies for the Rebellion, Luke Skywalker and Leia are attacked by an Imperial Star Destroyer. Their damaged ship shakes violently in hyperspace, and when they drop to sublight they find themselves in the starless void beyond their galaxy. The only visible "star" proves to be a mysterious organic vessel that swallows their transport whole. Inside, Luke and Leia are separated and attacked, Leia by tentacles and Luke by a lightsaber-wielding droid that treats the ordeal as a game.

When Luke defeats the droid, the entity controlling it panics on realizing the pair are real and tries to expel them into space. But when Luke refuses to release Leia's hand even at the risk of his own life, the entity understands that the two care for one another. It brings them to its control room and reveals its history: it was once a pilot and a mecho-organic warship, the sole survivors of a war whose enemy left behind a plague bomb that killed their entire population. The last survivor fled to the void, and over centuries the pilot and ship fused into one being that played games to stave off loneliness.

The ship, called Ship, agrees to return Luke and Leia home, but drops out of hyperspace into the same Star Destroyer that attacked them. It defends itself with anti-matter pods that annihilate the Destroyer and its TIE fighters. Deciding their galaxy is too much like the one it fled, Ship sends Luke and Leia on their way in their transport.
title: Star Wars (1977) #38: Riders in the Void
